summary: The diff implements most of the requested requirements. The Motoko backend covers user management, project management, lead management, post scheduling, AI text generation, image analysis, and dashboard analytics. The React frontend covers all screens with a dark-mode enterprise theme and bottom navigation. However, several specific requirements have gaps, particularly around the authentication mechanism, and some implementation details deviate from or extend the spec.

missing_requirements.requirement: loginUser returns a session token tied to the user and workspace; password field is stored
missing_requirements.reason: The LoginPage.tsx uses Internet Identity rather than email/password login. The registerUser mutation sends an empty hashedPassword string. The spec requires storing hashed passwords and a loginUser function returning a session token. The implementation replaces this with Internet Identity authentication, bypassing the password-based login flow entirely.

missing_requirements.requirement: Show email, password, role selector, and workspace ID fields; login returns a session token and redirects to Dashboard
missing_requirements.reason: The Login screen uses Internet Identity (a button-based OAuth-like flow) rather than an email+password form. There is no password field shown. The setup form shown after II authentication collects name, email, role, and workspaceId, but the primary login is not email/password based and does not produce a session token as specified.

missing_requirements.requirement: Logo image displayed in the login screen header; splash background used on login/register screen
missing_requirements.reason: The LoginPage.tsx references both image assets correctly, but no image files appear under frontend/public/assets/generated/ in the diff. Eight files were noted as omitted due to limits, so this cannot be fully confirmed, but evidence of the actual image assets being present is absent.

No VisionAnalyzer.tsx page file appears in the diff summary's listed files, though it is imported in App.tsx. The file may have been omitted due to the truncation limit of 8 omitted files, so this is flagged conservatively.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity (ICP) authentication system replacing email/password login
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec explicitly requires email/password login with a session token returned from loginUser. Instead, the implementation uses @dfinity/auth-client and Internet Identity for authentication, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Access control mixin and AccessControl module (MixinAuthorization, authorization/access-control)
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend imports and uses a MixinAuthorization and AccessControl module with permission-checking. The spec only requires role-based checks within the actor functions themselves with no mention of a separate access control framework.

hallucinated_features.description: isCallerAdmin() function exposed on the backend actor
hallucinated_features.reason: The frontend queries useIsCallerAdmin() which calls actor.isCallerAdmin(). This specific function was not listed in the spec's required exposed functions.

hallucinated_features.description: saveCallerUserProfile and getCallerUserProfile as primary profile storage pattern
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec defines getMe as the function to retrieve user info. The implementation uses getCallerUserProfile and saveCallerUserProfile instead, which is a different API surface not specified.

hallucinated_features.description: Prompt suggestion chips in the AI Generator screen
hallucinated_features.reason: The AI Generator page includes pre-filled prompt suggestion buttons. This UI feature was not requested in the spec.

hallucinated_features.description: Copy-to-clipboard button on AI Generator result
hallucinated_features.reason: The AI Generator screen includes a Copy button for the generated result. This was not part of the specified acceptance criteria.

hallucinated_features.description: TopBar component with logout button and user avatar
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec describes a bottom navigation bar but does not specify a top bar with logout and avatar. This is an added UI element beyond the spec.

hallucinated_features.description: _initializeAccessControlWithSecret called on actor initialization with a caffeineAdminToken URL parameter
hallucinated_features.reason: The actor hook calls _initializeAccessControlWithSecret with a token from URL parameters. This secret-based admin initialization pattern was not part of the build request.
